title: Frame Check MCP attribution schema
description: Per-resource and envelope-level attribution metadata exposed on every resources/list response from the frame-check-mcp server. Keys live under the reverse-DNS prefix `clarethium.com/` per MCP spec 2025-06-18 _meta key naming. Additive minor bumps (1.x.0) preserve consumer compatibility; breaking changes bump the major.

spdxLicense.description: SPDX license identifier. The Frame Check canon ships analytical artifacts under CC-BY-4.0; the source code is Apache-2.0 but is not advertised through this schema.

envelope.$comment: Schema for the resources/list response envelope's _meta block. The envelope-level attribution-schema-version pin lets a consumer negotiate evolution of this schema once per session rather than reading every resource entry.

clarethium.com/attribution-schema-versio.description: Pins the attribution-schema version this server emits. Consumers should fail-closed if the major component does not match the major they were built against.

analyticalArtifact.$comment: Per-resource _meta shape for analytical artifacts authored by the project (FVS library entries, transmissions, methodology, calibration runs, decision-readiness aggregates, worked examples). The CC-BY-4.0 quad applies. Transmissions additionally carry citation-uri sourced from the YAML frontmatter source_url; FVS entries that declare a per-entry version carry it as version.

clarethium.com/citation-uri.description: Optional. Human-readable canonical URL where the resource is published (e.g., the blog post URL for a transmission). Sourced from the resource's source-of-truth metadata (e.g., YAML frontmatter), not inferred from the URI slug.

clarethium.com/version.description: Optional. Per-entry version string, independent of any library-snapshot version. Pins the resource at a specific revision so consumers can cite without locking to the catalog as a whole.

annotations.description: MCP spec 2025-06-18 annotations (in addition to _meta). Transmissions emit lastModified for protocol-canonical drift detection. Other resource classes may emit additional annotations.

bundledDocument.$comment: Per-resource _meta shape for corpus URIs that ship verbatim third-party text or AI service outputs (frame-check://corpus/<slug> without a /profile, /peer/<partner>, or /diff/<partner> sibling segment). The CC-BY-4.0 attribution does not apply: the project did not author this content. Adopters must consult the document's in-file header for the actual origin and license. The analytical commentary at the sibling URIs continues to use the analyticalArtifact shape.

clarethium.com/license-note.description: Free-text pointer to where the actual licensing terms for this bundled document live (typically the document's in-file header).

examples.$comment: Envelope-level pin on a resources/list response.
Per-resource _meta on a bundled-document corpus URI.

_meta.clarethium.com/license-note: Bundled documents may contain verbatim third-party text under fair-use posture for research analysis, or AI-generated outputs from third-party services. See the document's in-file header for the accurate origin and copyright. Frame Check's analytical commentary on this document (CC-BY-4.0, Lovro Lucic) lives at the sibling /profile, /peer/<partner>, and /diff/<partner> URIs.
